text:
Keely Hodgkinson has revealed that she enjoyed an alcohol-laden party after winning herOlympicgold medal in the women’s 800m final. The young Brit was working on just two hours of sleep the following day.

The 22-year-old middle-distance runner was the overwhelming favourite heading into the final with American star Athing Mu absent from the Games altogether, and Hodgkinson duly delivered on her billing, storming through on the final lap to take gold, despite a charge from Kenya’s Mary Moraa.

Offering fans an insight into the moments that followed her historic triumph, Hodgkinson toldBBCSport: “So the first thing I did was actually anti-doping, so I went and weed in front of a random person!

“After that, I checked my phone, did a bit of a cooldown. I went to the press conference… it’s late at night at this point, it’s about 11:30 [pm] or 12, [so] you are getting quite tired. Then after that, I was fortunate enough to go and see my family and friends in a really cool bar in Paris.

“And there was over 40 people here supporting me that I know so that was really great, and I was there until about two or three in the morning. They were chanting, screaming, throwing drinks, having the most insane time.



Our community members are treated to special offers, promotions, and adverts from us and our partners. You can check out at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

“It’s really nice to know that what I achieved yesterday put a simple on their face and I could really enjoy the moment with them all. After that, I went home to bed, finally got to sleep at around half four.

“After that, I was up at 6:45 am on two hours sleep doing media in the morning for Team GB. I did about five hours of media and interviews there and talking about the night before fresh off the track basically.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

“Then I went home and I went to sleep, had a bit of a nap to go and get ready for my medal ceremony at 6:30 the next day. That was a really exciting and fun experience. I wanted to look nice, did my hair, did my make-up and went down to the track and really enjoyed a special moment.”

Hogkinson’s celebrations were well-deserved, to say the least. The Atherton-born athlete became the first female British winner in the 800m discipline since Kelly Holmes achieved the feat in 2004.

title: Team GB gold medallist had boozy party and only slept two hours before next commitment | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Harry Smith
description: One Team GB athlete enjoyed some wild celebrations after delivering a gold medal in Paris.
